data_description: Dataset description: a CSV table named `pendulum.csv` with columns (time_seconds, angle_radians) recording the small-amplitude oscillations of a 1.0-meter compound pendulum sampled at 200 Hz over 60 seconds. The pendulum was released from rest at an initial angle of 0.10 rad inside a temperature-controlled vacuum chamber so air drag is negligible. The pivot has a measured friction torque coefficient of 1.5e-4 N·m·s/rad. Tools available: numpy, scipy.optimize, scipy.signal, matplotlib. Goal: (i) extract the natural frequency f0 and quality factor Q of the pendulum from the time series, (ii) compare the measured period against the small-angle theoretical prediction T = 2π√(I/(mgL)) using the manufacturer's reported moment of inertia I = 0.083 kg·m² and pendulum length L = 0.50 m, and (iii) produce a residual plot to validate the simple-harmonic-oscillator (SHO) model and quantify any anharmonic deviation as a function of amplitude.

gold_sources.note: Nelson & Olsson 1986 — "The pendulum: rich physics from a simple system"
Lima 2018 — pendulum period beyond small-angle approximation
Sometimes-cited preprint on quality-factor extraction from damped oscillators
